task: I had a minor car issue come up and I’m trying to make sure I’d handle it the right way if it turned into an insurance thing. Can you help me make a simple checklist of what to do and what details to save so I’m not scrambling later?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a simple, glove-box-friendly checklist you can use for a minor fender-bender or small car incident.

## What to do right away
- **Stay calm and check for injuries**
- **Move the cars only if it’s safe and traffic is an issue**
- **Turn on hazard lights**
- **Call police if required or if there’s any dispute/injury**
- **Do not admit fault right away**

## What to document
Take photos before anyone leaves or talks too much:
- **Damage to all vehicles**
- **License plates**
- **Overall scene**
- **Road conditions / traffic signs / skid marks**
- **Any visible injuries**

Also save:
- **Names of all drivers**
- **Phone numbers**
- **Insurance company names**
- **Policy numbers if available**
- **Driver’s license numbers if you can get them**
- **Vehicle make/model/color**
- **Date, time, and exact location**
- **Police report number and agency name** if police respond

## For insurance
Write down a brief statement of:
- **What happened**
- **Direction each car was traveling**
- **Weather / road conditions**
- **Whether the car was moving or parked**

If a police report is made, keep:
- **Report number**
- **Responding agency**
- **Officer name or badge number if provided**

## Good thing to keep in the glove box
- Insurance card
- Registration
- A one-page checklist
- Pen
- Paper
- Your policy number and claims number

If you want, I can turn this into a **super short one-page printable checklist** you can save in your glove box.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, this works. Clear and practical, which is what I wanted. I’d keep it this simple and print it out.

One thing I’d probably add for myself: note whether you had a dashcam running and save the clip right away, because that’s saved me from relying on memory before. Also maybe take a photo of the other car’s VIN if you can get it, not just the plate.

But overall, the checklist is solid.
